title: Academic / Analytical
bestFor: Literary analysis, history essays, and close-reading tasks.
teachMoves: Ask what the reader needs to understand first
Check that each paragraph advances one claim
practiceScaffold: Students build a thesis, then assemble one AXES paragraph before drafting the whole essay.

title: Persuasive / Argumentative
bestFor: Position papers, op-eds, debate prep, and claim-evidence reasoning.
teachMoves: Force evidence into analysis, not summary
practiceScaffold: Students choose a claim, rank reasons, build a quote sandwich, and revise for reader objections.

title: Research
practiceScaffold: Students sort sources by role, then practice quote sandwiches and synthesis transitions.

title: Creative / Narrative
bestFor: Personal narratives, application essays, and fiction-inflected school writing.
teachMoves: Turn vague feelings into visible actions
practiceScaffold: Students draft a scene, mark characters/actions, then revise one paragraph for concrete stakes.

title: Compare / Contrast
bestFor: Paired texts, historical comparisons, and evaluation essays.
teachMoves: Make the comparison change the reader's view
practiceScaffold: Students rank comparison criteria, then build point-first body paragraphs.

title: Process / Explanatory
bestFor: How-to, scientific explanation, and concept-teaching assignments.
practiceScaffold: Students reorder steps, combine sentences, and revise for a reader who is new to the topic.

title: Critical Writing
teachMoves: Move from preference to reasoned evaluation
practiceScaffold: Students identify fallacies, build criteria, and revise claims for precise diction.
